semanticCore: Raindrops, rain showers, intimacy, closeness, or flash
quranicRelevance: Raindrops emerging from clouds (Wadq - 'yakhruju min khilalihi').

keyDerivations.meaning: Raindrops, rain showers, heavy precipitation, or water drops

figurativeOrRareUsages: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ يُزْجِي سَحَابًا ... فَتَرَى الْوَدْقَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلَالِهِ (24:43): Do you not see that God drives clouds... then you see the raindrops/showers emerging from their midst?
summaryEn: The root represents falling raindrops (Wadq), detailing the physics of clouds and the visual beauty of rain showers emerging from cloud formations.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر قطرات المطر المتساقطة المتتابعة من السحاب (ودق)، واصفاً تفجر قطرات المطر الغزيرة من خلال طبقات السحاب المتراكمة.
rawText: مُتَوَدِّقٌ : occurring in the TA, art. قهب; from الوَدْقُ, meaning Violent rain. (MF.) وَدِيقٌ : see 10 in art. قرأ. وَدْقٌ Rain, (S, K, TA,) whether violent or gentle: (TA:) or violent rain. (MF in art. قهب.)
